feed.title: Errant Thinking - RPG Blog Carnival
feed.subtitle: Asking insightful questions. Seeking thoughtful answers.

entry.title: Tiny Epics RPG Blog Carnival Round Up
entry.summary: As the March of the Tiny Epics comes to a close today, I give one last "Hurrah!" for all those who participated throughout this month. Thank you everyone for participating and sharing your wonderful TTRPG creations and reflections on the theme of "Tiny Epics: Small Souls in a Big World!". You all had great things to share and I'm glad to have read each and everyone of them. Let's celebrate the participants and their creations!

entry.title: Thoughts on BAYOU Roots
entry.summary: Palleon Press successfully got me monologuing too much in my March of the Tiny Epics overview so here that is to preserve the overview's flow. I clearly have some thoughts on BAYOU's Roots. Good thoughts, and thoughts on what others consider when characterizing their characters in TTRPGs, at least from a general level probably with too skewed a recollection from the popular titles.

entry.title: March of Tiny Epics
entry.summary: More have joined in on the March of "Tiny Epics!" for the RPG Blog Carnival. This community event is ongoing, and I wanted to discuss some people's shared creations! Share your creations with me early, and I'll do weekend updates in addition to the final roundup on March 31st.

entry.title: Amidst Tiny Epics
entry.summary: We're midway through March and already people have shared their creations for this month's RPG Blog Carnival on the theme "Tiny Epics: Small Souls in a Big World!". This community event is ongoing, and I wanted to discuss some people's published content as well as some discussion that occurred around the internet. Share your creations with me early, and I'll do weekend updates in addition to the final roundup on March 31st.

entry.title: Tiny Epics: Small Souls in a Big World!
entry.summary: The small ones among us are sometimes overlooked, but they surely have grand tales to tell! This March, let's explore these tiny epics for the RPG Blog Carnival. This is a community event, so we'll ponder some ideas for this theme, then get to creating and start some discussion! Share your creations with me early and I'll do weekend updates along with a final roundup on March 31st.

entry.title: Inspiring Real World Feasts and Festivals
entry.summary:
I've decided to join in the festivities of this November's RPG Blog Carnival. This month's theme is "Feasts and Festivals" in tabletop role-playing games as hosted by of Dice and Dragons.

I will discuss some real world festivals and mythology as sources of inspiration for one of my custom settings, Ophelos.
